outfront-gfx-kit.title: OUTFRONT GFX Kit
about: A centralized, cloud-hosted library of After Effects brand templates built so OUTFRONT's creative and sales teams could generate on-brand motion content without starting from scratch.

Each template is structured for fast turnaround — swap in new copy, imagery, or data and export broadcast-ready assets in minutes. The kit scales a single creative system across dozens of teams and campaigns.
embeds: <iframe src='https://player.vimeo.com/video/1177170199?title=0&byline=0&portrait=0&badge=0&autopause=0&player_id=0&app_id=58479' width='100%' height='100%' frameborder='0' allow='autoplay; fullscreen; picture-in-picture; clipboard-write; encrypted-media; web-share' referrerpolicy='strict-origin-when-cross-origin' title='Martens Offset'></iframe>

halftxt.title: HALFTXT
about: A suite of client-side tools for converting images, GIFs, and video into ASCII art — built entirely in the browser, with no backend and no dependencies.

Includes a media converter and a frame-by-frame animator with export to WebM, MP4, HTML, and plain text, demonstrating end-to-end product design and front-end development outside the constraints of client work.
embeds: <iframe src='https://player.vimeo.com/video/1177170199?title=0&byline=0&portrait=0&badge=0&autopause=0&player_id=0&app_id=58479' width='100%' height='100%' frameborder='0' allow='autoplay; fullscreen; picture-in-picture; clipboard-write; encrypted-media; web-share' referrerpolicy='strict-origin-when-cross-origin' title='Martens Offset'></iframe>

google-imagine-if.title: Imagine If
about: Google DeepMind and XLabs turned NYC's transit system into a user-generated art exhibit. Five local artists used AI to transform hundreds of New Yorkers' ideas into video artworks for thousands of MTA screens.
The challenge: every video needed to run on both vertical and horizontal DOOH screens. Manual processing would have taken 12 hours per hundred videos.

I built an AI-powered solution from scratch. I created separate horizontal and vertical After Effects templates, then used JSON files to dynamically populate each video with the correct borough, artist credit, and submission prompttext. To scale the work, I used AI to write custom Mac shell scripts, After Effects extensions, and plugins that automated the entire pipeline — feeding every raw video into both templates simultaneously.
The result: processing a hundred videos in about one hour instead of one day, with each delivered in both formats. The campaign ran across all five boroughs and culminated in a Times Square finale.
embeds: <center><video width='100%' controls><source src='/assets/portfolio/imagine_if/imagine_if_pipeline_3.mp4' type='video/mp4'></video></center>

frontiers.title: Frontiers
about: Frontiers is an unproduced documentary series I developed and pitched on advertising and technology, featuring interviews with leading designers, technologists, and academics.
The identity is anchored in the concept of 'breakthrough', visually protrayed with translucent glass blocks. Low-key cinematography creates intimacy and gravitas.
embeds: <iframe src='https://player.vimeo.com/video/1104237305?title=0&byline=0&portrait=0&badge=0&autopause=0&player_id=0&app_id=58479' style='width:100%;height:100%;' frameborder='0' allow='autoplay; fullscreen; picture-in-picture' allowfullscreen></iframe>

excursions.title: Excursions
about: Excursions was a pandemic-era content series designed to give prospective advertisers a guided tour of specific markets served by OUTFRONT Media, from local transit routes to neighborhood-level audience insights.

As Creative Director, I led a full rebrand and graphics package for the series, drawing inspiration from the nostalgic artifacts of classic road trips: paper maps, handwritten notes, highway signage, and worn gas station receipts. This visual language extended from the logo down to every graphic element within each episode, creating a cohesive, warm, and tactile identity that felt like discovery rather than a sales pitch. Although the branding was never implemented, the work demonstrates my ability to build a complete, insight-driven visual system that transforms dry market data into a storytelling vehicle.
embeds: <center><video width='100%' controls><source src='/assets/portfolio/excursions/exc_demo.mp4' type='video/mp4'></video></center>

verizon-pipeline.title: Verizon
about: From 2012 to 2016, I led a team of three motion designers and editors producing content for nearly every screen across Verizon's flagship destination stores and over 5,000 retail locations nationwide.

To meet weekly deliverables of 50 to 100 videos across multiple formats and sizes, I built new scripts, expressions, and workflows in After Effects — then extended that system into a unified set of motion guidelines for Verizon's brand-wide rebrand.
embeds: <iframe src='https://player.vimeo.com/video/1177170199?title=0&byline=0&portrait=0&badge=0&autopause=0&player_id=0&app_id=58479' width='100%' height='100%' frameborder='0' allow='autoplay; fullscreen; picture-in-picture; clipboard-write; encrypted-media; web-share' referrerpolicy='strict-origin-when-cross-origin' title='Martens Offset'></iframe>

all-night-wrong.title: All Night Wrong
about: All Night Wrong is an upcoming comedy directed by Jason James, premiering at South by Southwest London on June 2, 2026. The film follows Gary and Ell, who meet on a blind date and end up stealing a killer's car — along with a dead body and $40,000.

It stars Maria Bakalova (Borat Subsequent Moviefilm) and Zach Cherry (Severance), alongside Emily Hampshire, Tyler Labine, and Ryan Beil. I designed the film's main title sequence, setting the tone before the story even starts.
embeds: <iframe src='https://player.vimeo.com/video/1177170199?title=0&byline=0&portrait=0&badge=0&autopause=0&player_id=0&app_id=58479' width='100%' height='100%' frameborder='0' allow='autoplay; fullscreen; picture-in-picture; clipboard-write; encrypted-media; web-share' referrerpolicy='strict-origin-when-cross-origin' title='Martens Offset'></iframe>

outfront-brand-film.title: OUTFRONT Brand Film
about: What began as a request for a high-energy hype film to build excitement at Cannes evolved into something more significant — a film that helped shape a new brand identity for OUTFRONT.

Rather than the typical montage of billboards and screens common to OOH media, I introduced double exposures as a visual through-line, and integrated AI-generated NeRF and Gaussian splatting to reframe existing footage in unexpected ways.
The result became a centerpiece of OUTFRONT's CEO's presentation at Cannes, and established a unified creative direction that continues to influence the company's brand communications today.
embeds: <iframe src='https://player.vimeo.com/video/1177170199?title=0&byline=0&portrait=0&badge=0&autopause=0&player_id=0&app_id=58479' width='100%' height='100%' frameborder='0' allow='autoplay; fullscreen; picture-in-picture; clipboard-write; encrypted-media; web-share' referrerpolicy='strict-origin-when-cross-origin' title='Martens Offset'></iframe>

city-in-motion.title: City in Motion
city-in-motion.roll: Creative Direction, Concept, Creative Strategy, Branding
about: The goal of City in Motion was to shift advertiser perception from transit as mass impressions to transit as a portfolio of diverse, desirable audiences. We started from the insight that a rider's choice of transit mode reveals their identity, lifestyle stage, and daily priorities—not just their commute. That insight led to the big idea: Every commuter has a different city in motion.

Under my direction, our internal team shot documentary-style profiles of real NYC commuters, including Cait, a subway-dependent creative juggling multiple gigs per day, and Vanessa, a Long Island parent who uses the LIRR for both work and weekend family trips into the city. I set the handheld, rhythmically edited visual language to match each mode's energy and art-directed the motion graphics to amplify each persona without distracting from their story.
The series gave advertisers emotional segmentation they could see and feel—proving not just how many people ride, but who rides, when, and why their brand belongs there.

converse-forever-chuck.title: Converse: Forever Chuck
about: Forever Chuck reintroduced the Chuck Taylor All Star to a new generation, fronted by a Gen Z roster including Sabrina Carpenter, Cole Sprouse, Taylor Hill, Alton Mason, Alisha Boe, Selah Marley, and Rowan Blanchard.

As Associate Director of Film + Motion at AKQA, I supervised post-production, including editorial and color, and managed the campaign's rollout, launching the spots first on YouTube and Instagram, then extending into Instagram ads, and finally into out-of-home, translating the campaign across each format and platform.
embeds: <center><video width='100%' controls><source src='/assets/portfolio/foreverchuck/converse-forever_chuck_forever_you.mp4' type='video/mp4'></video></center>

maserati.title: Maserati
about: At AKQA, I led the launch and ongoing content for Maserati's US social media program.
Partnering with Rally & Co., I helped create the films that introduced the Maserati Levante, the brand's first SUV, to the North American market.
embeds: <

creative-commons-remix.title: Creative Commons Remix
about: Art direction and motion graphic design for the launch video introducing Creative Commons' 2016–2020 Organizational Strategy, debuted at an event at MoMA.

Released under a Creative Commons Attribution-ShareAlike license, the project was produced in direct collaboration with Creative Commons' CEO to define the visual language for the organization's new strategic direction.
embeds: <iframe src='https://player.vimeo.com/video/1177170199?title=0&byline=0&portrait=0&badge=0&autopause=0&player_id=0&app_id=58479' width='100%' height='100%' frameborder='0' allow='autoplay; fullscreen; picture-in-picture; clipboard-write; encrypted-media; web-share' referrerpolicy='strict-origin-when-cross-origin' title='Martens Offset'></iframe>

olympus.title: Olympus
about: Motion Graphics Director across multiple global launch campaigns for Olympus at AKQA, including the brand's global website relaunch and several flagship product debuts.

"Never Stop" became the basis for a global ad spot; "Share Beautifully" was translated into 18 languages and drove the highest-performing PEN camera launch to date; and the OM-D E-M10 spot introduced the brand's latest mirrorless model worldwide.
embeds: <iframe src='https://player.vimeo.com/video/1177170199?title=0&byline=0&portrait=0&badge=0&autopause=0&player_id=0&app_id=58479' width='100%' height='100%' frameborder='0' allow='autoplay; fullscreen; picture-in-picture; clipboard-write; encrypted-media; web-share' referrerpolicy='strict-origin-when-cross-origin' title='Martens Offset'></iframe>

must-have-squared.title: #MustHaveSquared
about: Wheat Thins makes the things you love better. For this social campaign with AKQA, we shot and directed a series of spots celebrating the snack's role in the audience's favorite activities.

The creative approach was simple and surreal: static blender shots combined Wheat Thins with activities pulled directly from the #MustHaveSquared hashtag, creating whimsical, snack-based objects that enhance beloved pastimes. The visual language was clean, product-forward, and playful, elevating the brand's warm, approachable tone while feeling native to social.

clearing.title: Clearing
about: Clearing is a web app and minimal iOS dashboard concept built around a single idea: every time you check the weather, your phone pulls you somewhere else. Clearing gives you what you came for, and nothing more.
Concepted and executed in roughly two days, the project is an exercise in product design, branding, storytelling, and positioning — end to end, solo.
embeds: <iframe src='https://player.vimeo.com/video/1177170199?title=0&byline=0&portrait=0&badge=0&autopause=0&player_id=0&app_id=58479' width='100%' height='100%' frameborder='0' allow='autoplay; fullscreen; picture-in-picture; clipboard-write; encrypted-media; web-share' referrerpolicy='strict-origin-when-cross-origin' title='Martens Offset'></iframe>

aruba-effect.title: The Aruba Effect
about: For Aruba Tourism, working with OUTFRONT XLabs, I helped develop QRad — a proprietary technology letting audiences interact with a massive Times Square billboard through a simple QR scan.

Passersby could scan the billboard and instantly take control of the screen based on their own vacation vibe, turning a passive impression into a live, participatory brand moment — proving DOOH could be both contextual and interactive at city scale.
embeds: <iframe src='https://player.vimeo.com/video/1177170199?title=0&byline=0&portrait=0&badge=0&autopause=0&player_id=0&app_id=58479' width='100%' height='100%' frameborder='0' allow='autoplay; fullscreen; picture-in-picture; clipboard-write; encrypted-media; web-share' referrerpolicy='strict-origin-when-cross-origin' title='Martens Offset'></iframe>

space-invaders-world-defense.title: Space Invaders: World Defense
about: For the 45th anniversary of Space Invaders, XLabs partnered with Google and Unit9 to launch a mobile game powered by ARCore and Google's geospatial API, anchored by a citywide Times Square activation.
The campaign unfolded in three phases — anticipation billboards, a live launch event, and neighborhood leaderboards fueled by real user data — turning New York City into a living game board.
Space Invaders: World Defense won Platinum at the Out of Home Advertising Association of America's 82nd OBIE Awards — the first Platinum win in OUTFRONT's 86-year history.
embeds: <iframe src='https://player.vimeo.com/video/1177170199?title=0&byline=0&portrait=0&badge=0&autopause=0&player_id=0&app_id=58479' width='100%' height='100%' frameborder='0' allow='autoplay; fullscreen; picture-in-picture; clipboard-write; encrypted-media; web-share' referrerpolicy='strict-origin-when-cross-origin' title='Martens Offset'></iframe>

snowfox.title: The Snowfox
about: The Snow Fox is a customizable digital storybook app that uses Siri to animate the story to a child's voice — featured by Apple as one of its "New Apps We Love" in the UK App Store.

I led the scenic animation, visual effects compositing for the launch film, and full production of the case study film, on a project that won a Cannes Lions Bronze Lion, a One Show Bronze Pencil, and two ADC Bronze Cubes.
embeds: <iframe src='https://player.vimeo.com/video/1177170199?title=0&byline=0&portrait=0&badge=0&autopause=0&player_id=0&app_id=58479' width='100%' height='100%' frameborder='0' allow='autoplay; fullscreen; picture-in-picture; clipboard-write; encrypted-media; web-share' referrerpolicy='strict-origin-when-cross-origin' title='Martens Offset'></iframe>

written-in-the-stars.title: Written in the Stars
about: Written in the Stars is a holiday digital experience that let users send and receive messages in the heavens, set to a soundtrack composed by 3D of Massive Attack.
I directed the motion graphics team behind the typographic animations and promotional films — and watched two real proposals get revealed live in the stars.
The project won a Cannes Lions Silver Lion in Mobile and a One Show Silver Pencil in Interactive.
embeds: <iframe src='https://player.vimeo.com/video/1177170199?title=0&byline=0&portrait=0&badge=0&autopause=0&player_id=0&app_id=58479' width='100%' height='100%' frameborder='0' allow='autoplay; fullscreen; picture-in-picture; clipboard-write; encrypted-media; web-share' referrerpolicy='strict-origin-when-cross-origin' title='Martens Offset'></iframe>

verizon-pulse.title: Verizon: Pulse
about: For Verizon's flagship Chicago store, I partnered with Moment Factory to create Pulse, the main film for a 40-foot LED wall spanning the store's atrium.

The work extended into Stories of Innovation, a companion series produced with the Chicago History Museum and Shotopop, each featurette exploring a different facet of the city's history of innovation.
embeds: <iframe src='https://player.vimeo.com/video/1177170199?title=0&byline=0&portrait=0&badge=0&autopause=0&player_id=0&app_id=58479' width='100%' height='100%' frameborder='0' allow='autoplay; fullscreen; picture-in-picture; clipboard-write; encrypted-media; web-share' referrerpolicy='strict-origin-when-cross-origin' title='Martens Offset'></iframe>
